resources.string: Connect to your PC, calibrate once, and start controlling the cursor.
Turn your phone into a wireless air mouse using motion sensors.
Rotate, tilt, and move naturally to control your PC.
Scan a QR code or enter IP address to connect instantly.
Voice commands, custom gestures, statistics, and more.
Calibration needed. Open Calibration and follow the steps.
Calibrated. Enter the PC IP and press Start.
Logs will appear here as the app runs.
Follow each step carefully. Keep the phone steady when asked, and move it in the requested pattern only during the measuring step.
Step 1/3: Keep device still for gyro
Lay the phone flat on a table and keep your hands off it while the gyroscope bias is measured.
Rotate the phone smoothly in a wide figure‑8 pattern so the magnetometer can see all directions.
Place the phone flat again and keep it still so the accelerometer can calibrate gravity.
Tap Start Guided Calibration to begin. The app will show each step and update the progress bar.
Use this if your device does not have a magnetometer. The app will continue in 6-axis mode.
Could not open the calibration screen.
Calibration complete. Return to Home and press Start.
Calibration failed. Check the instructions and try again.
Keep phone still for gyro bias…
Rotate phone in a figure‑8 for 15s…
Disable battery optimisation for stable operation
Sensor busy. Please restart the app.
Permission denied. Please grant the required permissions.
Position the QR code inside the frame
Camera permission is required to scan server QR codes.
Tap any card to expand or collapse its instructions.
Everything you need to know about Air Mouse Pro

1. Install the Air Mouse app on your Android phone.

2. Download the desktop server from our website.

3. Run the server and note its IP address.

4. Open the app, enter the IP and port (default 8080).

5. Tap Connect and start moving your phone.

6. The cursor will follow your phone orientation.

7. Calibrate sensors for best accuracy (see below).

• Both devices must be on the same Wi‑Fi network.

• Firewalls may block the connection – allow port 8080.

• Use the QR code scanner to auto‑configure IP/port.

• Wi‑Fi Direct is supported for direct peer‑to‑peer.

• Bluetooth mouse mode: pair your phone as a Bluetooth mouse.

• For best performance, use 5GHz Wi‑Fi with strong signal.

• Reconnect automatically after network drops.

Gyroscope Calibration:
• Place phone on a flat, stationary surface.
• Keep it still during the 45‑second collection.
• Removes gyro drift and improves tracking.

Accelerometer Calibration:
• Follow the 6 positions shown on screen.
• Hold each position still until the bar fills.
• Aligns gravity vector for accurate tilt.

Magnetometer Calibration:
• Move phone in a figure‑8 pattern.
• Continue until progress reaches 100%.
• Compensates for magnetic interference.

Quick twist (fast rotation) = Left click. Double twist = Double click. Hold tilt and flick = Right click. Fast vertical move = Scroll up/down. Custom gestures: record your own in the Gesture Studio. Voice commands: say click, scroll, right click (requires mic permission). Adjust sensitivity in Settings to match your style.

• AI Smoothing: Enables machine learning for ultra‑natural cursor movement.

• Predictive Movement: Kalman filter reduces perceived lag.

• Proximity Lock/Unlock: Automatically locks PC when you walk away (Bluetooth required).

• Auto‑Pause: Phone placed flat stops sending movement.

• Touchpad Mode: Use the phone as a touchpad.

• Profiles: Save different sensitivity settings for work, gaming, presentations.

• Statistics Dashboard: Tracks all gesture usage.

❌ Cannot connect:
 - Ensure both devices are on same network.
 - Disable VPN/firewall temporarily.
 - Try the QR code.

❌ Laggy cursor:
 - Reduce sensitivity (0.3–0.5).
 - Enable Predictive Movement.
 - Use 5GHz Wi‑Fi.

❌ Gestures not detected:
 - Recalibrate sensors.
 - Increase gesture sensitivity in Settings.
 - Train custom gestures with 5+ repetitions.

❌ Bluetooth mouse not working:
 - Ensure Bluetooth permission granted.
 - Pair phone as a mouse (check PC Bluetooth settings).

❌ App crashes:
 - Clear app cache.
 - Update to latest version.
 - Restart your phone.
Place device on a flat surface facing up
Gyroscope ready. Click start to continue.
Use two fingers to scroll, one finger to tap
Bluetooth permission required for HID mouse
Use the phone’s motion sensors to move the cursor and trigger clicks.
Use the phone like a trackpad for swipes, taps, and scrolling.
Enter your PC’s IP address or scan the QR code from the desktop app.
Run calibration after first setup for smoother cursor movement.
Use Bluetooth Mouse for local HID control when Wi‑Fi is not available.
Live motion feedback will appear here once Air Mouse is running.
Your clicks, scrolls, and other gestures appear here while you use the app.
Auto-pause motion when phone is flat
Warning: This will allow you to recalibrate sensors.

Air Mouse Pro is a graduate‑level embedded systems project that turns your smartphone into a smart, AI‑powered remote control for computers. It supports gesture recognition, proximity lock/unlock, predictive movement, and multi‑protocol connectivity (TCP, WebSocket, UDP, Bluetooth).

• Go (server) & Kotlin (Android)
• ONNX Runtime for AI smoothing
• TensorFlow Lite for on‑device gesture recognition
• Bluetooth Channel Sounding (BLE 6.0)
• Kalman filter for predictive movement

• Cross‑platform: Windows, macOS, Linux
• Multiple protocols: TCP, WebSocket, UDP, BLE
• AI: RNN for smoothing, CNN for gestures
• Battery‑optimised sensor processing
• Low latency (<20ms)
• Open‑source (MIT License)
MIT License – Free to use, modify, and distribute.
Adjust cursor speed to match how fast you move your hand.
No activity yet. Start the app and your recent actions will appear here.
No calibration attempts left. Restart the app.
Calibration attempts exhausted. Restart app to try again.
Bluetooth HID unsupported on this device
To act as a Bluetooth mouse, the app needs Bluetooth permission.

Quick Start

1. Install and run the Air Mouse server on your PC.
2. Make sure both devices are connected to the same Wi‑Fi network.
3. Enter the PC IP address or scan the QR code from the server.
4. Calibrate the sensors before the first session.
5. Tap Start Air Mouse and adjust sensitivity if needed.

Air Mouse Controls

• Move cursor: Rotate the phone smoothly to move the pointer.
• Left click: Perform a short quick flick gesture.
• Right click: Tilt the phone to the configured right-click angle.
• Double click: Perform two quick click gestures in succession.
• Scroll up/down: Move the phone vertically with a faster, shorter motion.

Practical Tips
• Keep the phone steady during calibration and while not actively moving the cursor.
• Start with medium sensitivity, then increase it only if the pointer feels slow.
• If gestures feel delayed, check Wi‑Fi quality and keep the phone close to the PC.
• Use the drawer to open calibration, statistics, logs, and the advanced tools.

Why Calibration Matters

Calibration removes sensor drift and makes cursor movement, clicks, and scrolling more reliable.

1. Gyroscope Calibration
Place the phone flat on a stable table and do not touch it while the app measures the bias offset.

2. Magnetometer Calibration
Slowly move the phone through a wide figure‑8 pattern so it can sample the surrounding magnetic field from multiple angles.

3. Accelerometer Calibration
Put the phone flat again and keep it still so the app can capture the gravity reference used for motion detection.

4. Completion
When the progress bar reaches 100%, return to the Home screen and press Start. If the room has strong magnetic noise, repeat calibration once more.

Device Sensors Used

• Gyroscope (3‑axis): Tracks angular velocity and powers the cursor movement and click gestures.
• Accelerometer (3‑axis): Detects linear motion, gravity direction, and scroll gestures.
• Magnetometer (3‑axis): Improves heading stability and reduces long-term drift when available.

How the app uses them
The app combines sensor streams with a Madgwick filter and gesture detectors so the cursor remains responsive while still filtering noise and tremor.

Missing sensors?
The app keeps working even if a sensor is not available, but some features will be simplified or disabled to preserve stability.

How to Connect to PC

1. On your PC
• Start the Air Mouse server application.
• Keep the server window open so it can accept connections.
• Copy the local IP address shown by the server or encode it in the QR code.

2. On your phone
• Connect to the same Wi‑Fi network as the PC.
• Enter the IP address manually or use the QR scanner to fill it automatically.
• Tap Start Air Mouse after calibration is complete.

Troubleshooting
• Verify that firewall rules allow the server port used by the desktop app.
• Make sure both devices are on the same subnet and that VPN is disabled while testing.
• If the connection fails, re-check the IP address and restart the server once.

Air Mouse Pro
Version %s

Turn your Android phone into a wireless air mouse using motion sensors, gesture recognition, and guided setup screens that are built for a complete end-to-end experience.

Developed by: Arian Firoozi, Arsalan Talaee
Instructors: Dr. Mohsen Shokri, Dr. Mehdi Kargahi
University of Tehran
Faculty of Electrical and Computer Engineering
Second Semester 1404-1405 (2025‑2026)

Open Source
This project is licensed under MIT. Source code available on GitHub.

Tap the button and speak a command
Speak one of the supported actions below, then review the result before sending another command.

• click
• double click
• right click
• scroll up
• scroll down
• calibrate
• start
• stop
Recording… Move the phone as you wish
Tap = click, long press = right click
Air Mouse Pro turns your phone into a wireless air mouse. Use natural hand movements to control your PC cursor, click, scroll, and more – no physical mouse needed.
Developed by: Arian Firoozi, Arsalan Talaee
Instructors: Dr. Mohsen Shokri, Dr. Mehdi Kargahi
University of Tehran – Faculty of Electrical and Computer Engineering

• Madgwick AHRS sensor fusion
• Material 3 design with dynamic theming
• QR code connection
• Voice commands & custom gestures
• Real‑time statistics
• Low‑latency UDP/TCP communication
This project is released under the MIT License. You are free to use, modify, and distribute it.
Scanning your local network for Air Mouse servers. This usually takes a few seconds.
Tap Scan to broadcast on the local network. Select a server to copy it into the Home screen.
Select a tab and follow the instructions to calibrate each sensor.
All sensors have been calibrated. You can now start the Air Mouse.
Progress will be lost. Are you sure?
Gyroscope: Keep the phone perfectly still…
Magnetometer: Move in a large figure‑8
Phone is moving! Keep it still and retry.
Calibration failed. Phone must be stationary.
Incorrect orientation. Please match the image.
Tap Retry to try this position again.
